message:
fix(deps,core): bump asupersync/ftui to crates.io releases and harden btrfs overflow paths

- Bump asupersync 0.2.0 → 0.2.5 and ftui 0.2.0 → 0.2.1, resolving from
  crates.io instead of stale local path overrides. Fixes rch remote worker
  build failures (bd-109v).
- Add checked_add for offset+length in btrfs_write and btrfs_fallocate to
  prevent u64 overflow on crafted inputs (bd-1pnz).
- Use saturating_add for next_objectid increments to avoid wrap-to-zero
  collision on pathological workloads.
- Apply rustfmt to ffs-dir test code.

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>

 {"id":"bd-1c4y.4","title":"Add unit tests for S3-FIFO eviction policy","description":"# Add unit tests for S3-FIFO eviction policy

## GOAL
Provide unit test coverage for the S3-FIFO epic (bd-1c4y). Currently has E2E test (bd-1c4y.3) and benchmark (bd-1c4y.2) but NO unit tests for the three-queue eviction logic, scan resistance, or promotion/demotion paths.

## TEST PLAN

### Queue Structure Tests
1. New entry goes to small queue (S)
2. Second access promotes from S to main queue (M)
3. Eviction from S goes to ghost queue (G)
4. Re-access from G promotes to M (scan resistance)
5. Queue size ratios maintained (S=10%, M=90%)

### Eviction Tests
6. Eviction selects from S first (FIFO order)
7. S empty: eviction falls through to M (FIFO order)
8. Ghost queue bounded (does not grow unbounded)
9. Cache at capacity: insert triggers eviction

### Scan Resistance Tests
10. Sequential scan does not pollute M queue
11. Hot entries survive sequential scan
12. Mixed workload: hot set retained, scan set evicted

### Integration with ARC
13. S3-FIFO can replace ARC eviction policy
14. Cache hit/miss metrics correct under S3-FIFO

## TESTS (REQUIRED)
All 14 tests above. Deterministic.

## LOGGING REQUIREMENTS
- Test failures log queue sizes and contents

## ACCEPTANCE CRITERIA
1. All 14 tests pass
2. Scan resistance verified (hot entries retained)
3.  {"id":"bd-1mdk.2","title":"Implement Bw-Tree consolidation (delta chain compaction)","description":"# Implement Bw-Tree consolidation (delta chain compaction)

## GOAL
Implement background consolidation that merges accumulated delta records back into a base page, preventing unbounded delta chain growth and maintaining lookup performance.

## BACKGROUND
From Alien CS Graveyard entry 8.7: Without consolidation, delta chains grow linearly with writes, degrading lookup from O(log n) to O(log n + chain_length). Consolidation creates a fresh base page incorporating all deltas, then atomically replaces the chain via CAS on the mapping table.

## DESIGN

### Consolidation Trigger
- Chain length threshold: consolidate when delta chain exceeds configurable max (default: 16 deltas)
- Background thread: periodic scan of all pages for chains exceeding threshold
- Opportunistic: reader that encounters long chain can trigger inline consolidation

### Consolidation Protocol
1. Pin epoch (prevent GC of chain during consolidation)
2. Traverse delta chain, collecting all Insert/Delete operations
3. Apply operations to base page, producing new consolidated base
4. CAS mapping_table[page_id] from old chain head to new base
5. On CAS success: defer_destroy old chain nodes
6. On CAS failure: discard new base, retry (new deltas appended concurrently)

### Split/Merge During Consolidation
- If consolidated page exceeds max size: split into two pages
- If consolidated page below min size and sibling below threshold: merge
- Split/Merge are themselves delta records, enabling concurrent structural modifications

### Structural Modification Protocol (SMO)
- Split: create new sibling page, append Split delta to original, append Insert delta to parent
- Merge: append Merge delta to removed page, append Delete delta to parent
- All via CAS — no locks, no latches

## RUST IMPLEMENTATION NOTES
- Consolidation can race with concurrent inserts: CAS handles this correctly
- crossbeam-epoch Guard must be held during entire consolidation
- New base page: Owned::new(PageDelta::Base(consolidated_node))
- Location: ffs-bwtree/src/consolidation.rs

## ACCEPTANCE CRITERIA
- [ ] Consolidation merges delta chain into fresh base page
- [ ] CAS atomically replaces old chain with consolidated base
- [ ] Concurrent writers during consolidation handled (CAS retry)
- [ ] Chain length bounded by consolidation threshold
- [ ] Split triggered when consolidated page too large
- [ ] Merge triggered when consolidated page too small
- [ ] Unit tests: consolidation produces correct base page
- [ ] Unit tests: concurrent consolidation + insert race
- [ ] Benchmark: lookup latency before/after consolidation
- [ ] Logging: tracing spans for consolidation trigger, duration, chain length before/after","status":"closed","priority":2,"issue_type":"task","assignee":"CoralReef","created_at":"2026-02-13T17:21:19.279073429Z","created_by":"ubuntu","updated_at":"2026-02-18T00:05:38.214945707Z","closed_at":"2026-02-18T00:05:38.214923155Z","close_reason":"Implemented Bw-Tree delta chain consolidation in bw_tree.rs: consolidate_page (materialize + CAS replace), chain_length, scan_for_consolidation, consolidate_all, ConsolidationConfig/ConsolidationResult types.  {"id":"bd-1mdk.3","title":"Add comprehensive unit tests for Bw-Tree operations","description":"# Add comprehensive unit tests for Bw-Tree operations

## GOAL
Build a thorough unit test suite verifying Bw-Tree correctness: single-threaded CRUD, concurrent operations, consolidation correctness, split/merge structural modifications, and memory safety (Miri-compatible).

## TEST CATEGORIES

### 1. Single-Threaded CRUD
- Insert 1000 keys, verify all lookups return correct values
- Delete half the keys, verify deleted keys return None
- Insert duplicate keys (update semantics), verify latest value wins
- Range scan: iterate keys in sorted order, verify ordering

### 2. Concurrent Insert/Lookup
- 4 threads each inserting 1000 unique keys concurrently
- After all threads complete: verify all 4000 keys present
- During inserts: 4 reader threads continuously looking up random keys
- Readers must never see partial/corrupt state

### 3. Concurrent Insert/Delete
- 2 writer threads: one inserting, one deleting (non-overlapping ranges)
- After completion: inserted range present, deleted range absent
- Overlap test: both threads target same keys, verify no lost updates

### 4. Consolidation Correctness
- Insert 100 deltas on one page, trigger consolidation
- Verify consolidated base page contains correct merged state
- Insert during consolidation: verify new delta visible after consolidation

### 5. Split/Merge
- Insert enough keys to trigger page split
- Verify both pages contain correct key ranges
- Delete keys to trigger merge, verify single page remains
- Concurrent split: multiple threads triggering splits on adjacent pages

### 6. Memory Safety (Miri)
- Small-scale tests (100 keys) runnable under Miri
- Verify no use-after-free, no data races, no undefined behavior
- crossbeam-epoch reclamation: verify old nodes freed after epoch advance

### 7. Edge Cases
- Empty tree: lookup, delete, range scan on empty tree
- Single-element tree: all operations
- Maximum chain length: verify consolidation triggers at threshold
- CAS retry exhaustion: verify graceful handling (if implemented)

## ACCEPTANCE CRITERIA
- [ ] All 7 test categories implemented and passing
- [ ] Concurrent tests use deterministic seeding for reproducibility
- [ ] Miri tests pass (no UB detected)
- [ ] Test coverage: >= 80% line coverage for bwtree module
- [ ] Each test has descriptive name and documents expected behavior
- [ ] Logging: test harness captures tracing output for debugging failures","status":"closed","priority":2,"issue_type":"task","assignee":"CoralReef","created_at":"2026-02-13T17:21:35.295345664Z","created_by":"ubuntu","updated_at":"2026-02-18T00:11:05.317085042Z","closed_at":"2026-02-18T00:11:05.317042362Z","close_reason":"Added 16 comprehensive Bw-Tree unit tests covering: single-threaded CRUD (1000 keys insert/delete/lookup), duplicate insert update semantics, sorted materialization, concurrent insert+lookup (4+4 threads), concurrent insert+delete (non-overlapping and overlapping), empty page ops, single-element ops, unallocated page error, page allocation exhaustion, consolidation of empty page, threshold-based scan and selective consolidate_all, multi-page independence.  {"id":"bd-1mdk.4","title":"E2E benchmark: Bw-Tree vs locked B-tree extent index throughput","description":"# E2E benchmark: Bw-Tree vs locked B-tree extent index throughput

## GOAL
Prove that the Bw-Tree extent index provides superior concurrent throughput compared to the current locked B-tree implementation. This is the justification benchmark for the entire Bw-Tree epic.

## BENCHMARK SCENARIOS

### Scenario 1: Read-Heavy (95% lookup, 5% insert)
- Simulate metadata-heavy workload (readdir, stat)
- Thread counts: 1, 2, 4, 8, 16
- Measure: lookups/sec, p50/p95/p99 lookup latency
- Expected: Bw-Tree scales linearly (readers never block); locked B-tree saturates

### Scenario 2: Write-Heavy (30% lookup, 70% insert/delete)
- Simulate file creation/deletion workload
- Thread counts: 1, 2, 4, 8, 16
- Measure: operations/sec, CAS retry rate, consolidation frequency

 {"id":"bd-2s4.5","title":"E2E test: SIGKILL during write, remount, verify consistency","description":"# E2E test: SIGKILL during write, remount, verify consistency

## GOAL
Verify that FrankenFS maintains consistency guarantees after an unclean shutdown (SIGKILL during active writes). The filesystem must be mountable, crash recovery must work, and all committed data must be intact.

## TEST SCENARIO

### Phase 1: Setup
1. Create fresh 256MB ext4 image
2. Mount via FUSE, write baseline dataset (500 files, checksums recorded)
3. fsync all files, verify baseline is durable
4. Begin continuous write workload (background thread writing new files)

### Phase 2: Crash
5. After 2 seconds of active writing, send SIGKILL to FUSE process
6. Record which files had fsync called vs which were in-flight

### Phase 3: Recovery
7. Remount the filesystem image
8. Crash recovery should trigger automatically (journal replay, MVCC rollback)
9. Record evidence ledger output

### Phase 4: Verification
10. All baseline files (pre-crash) must be intact with correct checksums
11. Files that had fsync completed must be intact
12. Files that were in-flight at SIGKILL time: acceptable to be missing or truncated, but:
    - Must NOT contain garbage data
    - Must NOT corrupt other files
    - Directory must be consistent (no dangling entries)
13. No filesystem-level corruption (fsck-equivalent check passes)

### Invariants
- Atomicity: a file write either fully committed or fully absent
- No cross-file corruption: crash during file A write must never affect file B
- Metadata consistency: directory entries match existing inodes
- Journal consistency: no partial journal transactions visible

## ACCEPTANCE CRITERIA
- [ ] Filesystem mounts after SIGKILL without errors
- [ ] All pre-crash baseline files intact with correct checksums
- [ ] All fsync'd files intact
- [ ] In-flight files are either complete or cleanly absent (no partial garbage)
- [ ] No cross-file corruption
- [ ] Evidence ledger records crash recovery actions
- [ ] Test is reproducible (deterministic write pattern)
- [ ] Test completes in < 60s","status":"closed","priority":1,"issue_type":"task","assignee":"CalmGorge","created_at":"2026-02-13T09:21:40.893316017Z","created_by":"ubuntu","updated_at":"2026-02-18T09:20:36.682290287Z","closed_at":"2026-02-18T09:20:36.682269679Z","close_reason":"Completed: SIGKILL E2E now passes with deterministic crash invariants","source_repo":".","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0,"labels":["crash","e2e","fuse","test"],"dependencies":[{"issue_id":"bd-2s4.5","depends_on_id":"bd-2s4","type":"parent-child","created_at":"2026-02-13T09:21:40.893316017Z","created_by":"ubuntu","metadata":"{}","thread_id":""},{"issue_id":"bd-2s4.5","depends_on_id":"bd-2s4.3","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-02-13T09:31:10.730235864Z","created_by":"ubuntu","metadata":"{}","thread_id":""},{"issue_id":"bd-2s4.5","depends_on_id":"bd-2s4.4","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-02-13T09:31:13.504998589Z","created_by":"ubuntu","metadata":"{}","thread_id":""},{"issue_id":"bd-2s4.5","depends_on_id":"bd-2s4.7","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-02-18T09:08:10.911533057Z","created_by":"CalmGorge","metadata":"{}","thread_id":""}],"comments":[{"id":134,"issue_id":"bd-2s4.5","author":"Dicklesworthstone","text":"Alien uplift contract (auto-applied via extreme-software-optimization + alien-artifact-coding + alien-graveyard).

 {"id":"bd-2s4.6","title":"Add comprehensive unit tests for FUSE dispatch and lifecycle","description":"# Add comprehensive unit tests for FUSE dispatch and lifecycle

## GOAL
Provide unit test coverage for the Production FUSE Runtime epic (bd-2s4). Currently the epic has E2E tests (bd-2s4.4, bd-2s4.5, bd-3en1) but NO dedicated unit tests for the dispatch layer, lifecycle management, or errno mapping.

## TEST PLAN

### 1. Dispatch Tests
- Thread pool spawns correct number of threads
- Concurrent requests are dispatched without deadlock
- Request timeout fires and returns EIO
- Queue depth metrics are updated correctly

### 2. Lifecycle Tests
- Mount initializes all subsystems in correct order
- Unmount flushes dirty data before teardown
- SIGTERM triggers clean unmount sequence
- Double-unmount is idempotent (no panic)
- Mount with invalid image returns descriptive error

### 3. Errno Mapping Tests
- Every FfsError variant maps to correct errno
- Unknown errors map to EIO
- Cancelled maps to EINTR
- CorruptData maps to EIO
- NoSpace maps to ENOSPC
- PermissionDenied maps to EACCES

### 4. State Tests
- FsOps state is Send + Sync (compile-time test)
- Concurrent getattr calls do not race
- Cache access under lock is bounded (no starvation)

## TESTS (REQUIRED)
1. All tests listed above
2. Total: ~20 unit tests
3. All deterministic, no I/O (mock BlockDevice)

## LOGGING REQUIREMENTS
- Test failures log full context (operation, expected, actual)

## ACCEPTANCE CRITERIA
1. All dispatch, lifecycle, errno, and state tests pass
2. No flaky tests (deterministic)
3.  {"id":"bd-2s4.7","title":"Fix ext4 RW write persistence across clean unmount/remount","description":"## Summary
In privileged E2E runs, ext4 RW operations succeed, but data written in phase 3 is missing after clean unmount + RO remount.

## Repro
1. Build `ffs-cli` (or use prebuilt binary).
2. Run:
   `sudo -n env PATH="$PATH" FFS_SKIP_BUILD=1 FFS_CLI_BIN=/data/projects/frankenfs/target/release/ffs-cli FFS_AUTO_UNMOUNT=0 ./scripts/e2e/ffs_ext4_rw_smoke.sh`
3. Observe failure in phase 4: `persist.txt` is missing on RO remount.

## Evidence
- Run artifact: `artifacts/e2e/20260218_040650_ffs_ext4_rw_smoke/run.log`
- RO mount log: `artifacts/e2e/20260218_040650_ffs_ext4_rw_smoke/mount_ro_mnt_ro.log`

## Expected
Writes acknowledged in RW mount (and fsync-backed sentinel writes) remain visible after clean unmount/remount.

## Actual
`persist.txt` is readable in RW mount, but absent after remount.

## Notes
- Prior RW EIO mount failure appears resolved in this environment.
- Unmount robustness was improved in `scripts/e2e/lib.sh`, so this now consistently surfaces a persistence/durability issue rather than mount teardown noise.","status":"closed","priority":0,"issue_type":"bug","assignee":"CalmGorge","created_at":"2026-02-18T09:07:52.623298157Z","created_by":"CalmGorge","updated_at":"2026-02-18T09:18:40.625109148Z","closed_at":"2026-02-18T09:18:40.625071358Z","close_reason":"Completed: fixed RW persistence loss by using Apply replay mode for RW mount","source_repo":".","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0,"labels":["durability","ext4","fuse"],"dependencies":[{"issue_id":"bd-2s4.7","depends_on_id":"bd-2s4","type":"parent-child","created_at":"2026-02-18T09:07:52.623298157Z","created_by":"CalmGorge","metadata":"{}","thread_id":""}],"comments":[{"id":238,"issue_id":"bd-2s4.7","author":"Dicklesworthstone","text":"Resolved root cause in CLI mount path: `mount_cmd` was always opening ext4 with `Ext4JournalReplayMode::SimulateOverlay`, which made RW writes session-local and non-persistent.

 {"id":"bd-2tt","title":"Implement ext4 orphan inode recovery","description":"# Implement ext4 orphan inode recovery

## GOAL
Implement ext4 orphan inode list processing as required by FEATURE_PARITY.md: "ext4 orphan recovery parity: ❌ Not yet implemented"

## BACKGROUND
ext4 maintains an orphan inode list for crash recovery:
- Inodes being deleted but still open
- Truncated files with pending block release
- On mount, these must be cleaned up

## TECHNICAL REQUIREMENTS

### 1. Orphan List Structure
```rust
/// ext4 orphan inode linked list
/// s_last_orphan in superblock -> first orphan inode
/// i_dtime in orphan inode -> next orphan (0 = end)
pub struct OrphanList {
    head: InodeNumber,
}

impl OrphanList {
    pub fn read(sb: &Ext4Superblock, device: &dyn BlockDevice) -> Result<Self>;
    pub fn iter(&self) -> OrphanIter;
}
```

### 2. Recovery Actions
```rust
pub fn recover_orphan(
    inode_num: InodeNumber,
    fs: &mut OpenFs,
    cx: &Cx,
) -> Result<OrphanAction> {
    let inode = fs.read_inode(inode_num)?;
    
    if inode.link_count == 0 {
        // Deleted file, reclaim blocks and inode
        reclaim_inode_blocks(inode_num, fs, cx)?;
        free_inode(inode_num, fs, cx)?;
        Ok(OrphanAction::Deleted)
    } else {
        // Truncated file, fix size
        truncate_to_size(inode_num, inode.size, fs, cx)?;
        Ok(OrphanAction::Truncated)
    }
}
```

### 3. Mount Integration
- Process orphan list before journal replay
- Clear orphan list after processing
- Log actions taken

### 4. Error Handling
- Invalid orphan inode -> log and skip
- Circular list -> detect and break
- I/O error -> mount read-only

## TESTS
1. Unit: Parse orphan list from superblock
2. Integration: Orphan with link_count=0 deleted
3. Integration: Orphan with link_count>0 truncated
4. Integration: Corrupt orphan list handled gracefully

## LOGGING
- Orphan list head (debug)
- Each orphan processed (info): inode, action
- Orphan list cleared (info)

## ACCEPTANCE CRITERIA
1. All orphans processed on mount
2. Blocks correctly reclaimed
3. No orphan references remain
4. Mount succeeds after crash","status":"closed","priority":2,"issue_type":"task","created_at":"2026-02-13T02:53:00.165065314Z","created_by":"ubuntu","updated_at":"2026-02-17T08:02:09.633627927Z","closed_at":"2026-02-17T08:02:09.633602790Z","close_reason":"Implemented mount-time ext4 orphan recovery (delete/truncate actions + superblock orphan-state clearing), added tests, and passed rch-offloaded gates.","source_repo":".","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0,"labels":["ext4","mount","recovery"],"dependencies":[{"issue_id":"bd-2tt","depends_on_id":"bd-1xe","type":"parent-child","created_at":"2026-02-13T03:56:40.727004221Z","created_by":"ubuntu","metadata":"{}","thread_id":""},{"issue_id":"bd-2tt","depends_on_id":"bd-1xe.3","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-02-13T03:50:17.164101964Z","created_by":"ubuntu","metadata":"{}","thread_id":""},{"issue_id":"bd-2tt","depends_on_id":"bd-1xe.4","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-02-13T04:33:40.839125543Z","created_by":"ubuntu","metadata":"{}","thread_id":""},{"issue_id":"bd-2tt","depends_on_id":"bd-huh.2","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-02-13T04:33:40.739984385Z","created_by":"ubuntu","metadata":"{}","thread_id":""}],"comments":[{"id":29,"issue_id":"bd-2tt","author":"Dicklesworthstone","text":"FEATURE_PARITY mapping: This covers 'ext4 orphan recovery parity' (currently ❌).  {"id":"bd-xzn0","title":"Succinct structures for free-space bitmaps (Graveyard Entry 7.1)","description":"# Succinct structures for free-space bitmaps (Graveyard Entry 7.1)

## GOAL
Replace traditional free-space bitmaps with succinct data structures (rank/select bitmaps) that support O(1) rank and select queries while using near-optimal space.

## BACKGROUND
From Alien CS Graveyard 7.1: Succinct bitmaps (Jacobson 1989, Clark 1996) store bitmaps in n + o(n) bits while supporting:
- rank(i): count set bits in positions [0, i) — O(1)
- select(k): find position of k-th set bit — O(1)

For free-space bitmaps, this enables:
- O(1) "how many free blocks in range?" (rank)
- O(1) "find k-th free block" (select) — replaces linear scan for allocation

## DESIGN
- Two-level rank structure: superblocks (every 2^16 bits) + blocks (every 2^9 bits)
- Select via rank binary search or Vigna's select structure
- Space overhead: ~6.25% over raw bitmap (vs 0% for plain bitmap, but queries are O(n))
- Integration: replace ffs-ext4 block bitmap and inode bitmap

## APPLICABILITY
- Optimization for large filesystems where bitmap scans are expensive
- Most impactful when block groups are large or filesystem is >90% full
- Could also accelerate mballoc buddy allocator queries

## ACCEPTANCE CRITERIA
- [ ] Succinct bitmap with O(1) rank and select
- [ ] Space overhead < 10% over raw bitmap
- [ ] Benchmark: allocation scan time vs plain bitmap
- [ ] Drop-in replacement for block/inode bitmaps","acceptance_criteria":"Succinct bitmap with O(1) rank and select operations. Space overhead below 10% over raw bitmap. Two-level rank structure: superblocks (every 2^16 bits) plus blocks (every 2^9 bits). Drop-in replacement for block and inode bitmaps via BlockBitmap trait. Benchmark: allocation scan time vs plain bitmap at 50%, 80%, 95% fullness. Benchmark: rank/select latency vs linear scan. Unit tests: rank correctness for all-zeros, all-ones, random patterns. Unit tests: select correctness for sparse and dense bitmaps. Unit tests: round-trip serialization/deserialization. Property test: rank(select(k)) == k for all valid k.","status":"closed","priority":3,"issue_type":"task","assignee":"CoralThrush","created_at":"2026-02-13T09:30:45.958783809Z","created_by":"ubuntu","updated_at":"2026-02-18T06:30:55.736127822Z","closed_at":"2026-02-18T06:30:55.736029688Z","source_repo":".","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0,"labels":["datastructure","freespace","succinct"],"dependencies":[{"issue_id":"bd-xzn0","depends_on_id":"bd-3ib","type":"parent-child","created_at":"2026-02-13T18:05:32.714682331Z","created_by":"ubuntu","metadata":"{}","thread_id":""}],"comments":[{"id":202,"issue_id":"bd-xzn0","author":"Dicklesworthstone","text":"Alien uplift contract (auto-applied via extreme-software-optimization + alien-artifact-coding + alien-graveyard).
